result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The current bibliography covers systemic engineering challenges (Sculley et al.) and theoretical adversarial vulnerabilities (Ilyas et al.). However, there is a critical gap in addressing empirical, real-world distribution shifts and common corruptions that degrade model performance outside of controlled benchmarks. Adding a foundational paper on robustness to common corruptions (like ImageNet-C) will directly support arguments about the brittleness of deep learning models, provide a concrete methodology for evaluating real-world failure modes, and highlight the research gap between clean accuracy and out-of-distribution generalization. This aligns well with categories 4 (Comparing Findings), 5 (Highlighting Research Gaps), and 7 (Supporting Arguments).
